title: native: hello advertises send_message support even when handlers drop it
description: Code review finding from unreviewed Zephyr/C paths. lichen_native_send_hello() always advertises LN_TYPE_SEND_MESSAGE as supported, but the default native handler does not implement it, the gateway uses the default handler, and the puck handler intercepts send_message only to log that LoRa TX is not implemented. A host can therefore believe host-to-device mesh sends are supported and then have messages silently ignored. Evidence: native.c:407-415 includes LN_TYPE_SEND_MESSAGE in the advertised supported array; native.c:619-654 only handles HELLO and LOG_SUBSCRIBE; lichen/apps/gateway/src/main.c:421 passes lichen_native_handle_rx directly; lichen/apps/puck/src/main.c:88-91 logs not implemented for LN_TYPE_SEND_MESSAGE. Acceptance: either implement send_message routing/TX for each app that advertises it or make advertised capabilities app-specific so unsupported message types are omitted.
acceptance_criteria: After implementation, run the relevant tests/builds and then complete two separate code-review passes before closing. Each review pass should inspect the produced code against this Bead's acceptance criteria and file follow-up Beads for any discovered issues.
close_reason: Default native hello capabilities now match the native library's implemented device-side behavior: HELLO, MESSAGE_RECEIVED, NODE_INFO, LOG_ENTRY, and LOG_SUBSCRIBE. lichen_native_send_hello no longer advertises LN_TYPE_SEND_MESSAGE, LN_TYPE_CONFIG_GET, or LN_TYPE_CONFIG_RESULT, so gateway and puck callers that use the default hello no longer tell hosts that unsupported host-to-device send/config operations are available. Added a test-only hello encoder hook and decoder-backed zcbor assertions that the generated hello omits those unhandled commands while retaining supported native messages. Tests/builds: west build -b qemu_x86 lichen/tests/native_parse -d build/native_parse_qemu --pristine=always passed; west build -t run -d build/native_parse_qemu printed 14/14 pass and PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL before timeout stopped QEMU; west build -b nrf52840dk_nrf52840 lichen/apps/gateway -d build/gateway_nrf52840dk --pristine=always passed; git diff --check passed. Review pass 1: verified no advertised send_message/config entries remained in the default hello list and strengthened positive test coverage for retained capabilities. Review pass 2: verified remaining LN_TYPE_SEND_MESSAGE reference is only puck defensive handling for unexpected input, not advertisement. Follow-ups created for unrelated validation blockers: project-LICHEN-9nrr.21 for puck RAK4631 undefined lora0 overlay and project-LICHEN-9nrr.22 for puck T-Echo crc32_ieee link failure.
